critiqued_hypotheses.title: TST Affinity-Based Dissolution Model for Amorphous Solid Dispersions
critiqued_hypotheses.key_attack: ASD dissolution is predominantly diffusion-controlled (polymer swelling, chain disentanglement), not surface-reaction-controlled as TST assumes. Dove 2008 showed TST works for amorphous silica but that still involves Si-O bond hydrolysis, fundamentally different from polymer dissolution.
critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: Genuinely novel (0 TST in pharma ASD). Strong motivation (pharma lacks predictive mechanistic dissolution models). Survives because no one has tested whether TST applies to the drug-release component of ASD dissolution, and some dissolution regimes may be surface-reaction limited. Minor citation issue: spring-parachute coined by Guzmán 2007, not Ting 2018.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: Passivation Layer Kinetics Unify Glass Alteration Rinds and ASD Polymer-Rich Surface Layers
critiqued_hypotheses.key_attack: Glass alteration rinds are permanent, rigid, microporous barriers that suppress dissolution (D_eff ~10^-21 m²/s). ASD polymer-rich layers are transient, viscoelastic, and can dissolve/erode entirely, breaking parabolic kinetics. The shared sqrt(t) form is generic diffusion behavior (Higuchi 1961), not evidence of mechanistic connection. Citation error: Gin et al. 2015 published in Nature Communications, NOT Nature Materials.
critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: Novel mathematical framing with real sqrt(t) analogy, but the analogy is shallow — it arises from generic diffusion physics rather than shared mechanism. Polymer layer transience fundamentally breaks the glass rind model. Citation error on flagship reference (wrong journal) is concerning.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: PHREEQC-Style Speciation Modeling Predicts ASD Dissolution Phase Behavior
critiqued_hypotheses.key_attack: PHREEQC's core value is its validated inorganic thermodynamic database using Debye-Hückel activity coefficients. Drug-polymer systems require Flory-Huggins theory — fundamentally incompatible frameworks. Building an organic thermodynamic database for PHREEQC would require rewriting the activity coefficient engine and measuring thousands of interaction parameters, effectively building a new tool. Additionally, PHREEQC assumes equilibrium speciation while LLPS/crystallization are kinetic phenomena.
critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: N/A — killed. Novel in the trivial sense that no one has tried it, but novel because it's impractical, not because it's unexplored. Sun et al. 2016 citation for F-H chi parameters could not be specifically verified.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: Composition-Dissolution Rate Functions Enable Predictive ASD Formulation Screening
critiqued_hypotheses.key_attack: Severe dimensionality problem: silicate glasses vary in ~10 oxides, enabling 5-8 parameter regressions. Pharmaceutical drugs span millions of distinct organic structures requiring 10-20+ descriptors. This reduces to QSPR/QSAR with geochemical terminology. Pharma DOE already performs regression-based formulation optimization.
critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: The specific TST-based composition-rate mathematical formalism from geochemistry has not been applied to ASDs. Could work within a narrow application (single drug-polymer pair at varying ratios) but not across structurally diverse drugs. Essentially QSPR with extra steps.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: V-Shaped pH Dependence of ASD Dissolution Mirrors Silicate Glass
critiqued_hypotheses.key_attack: Decorative analogy. Glass V-shaped pH dependence arises from proton-catalyzed hydrolysis of Si-O-Al bonds (acid) and OH⁻ attack on Si-O-Si bonds (base). ASD pH dependence arises from drug ionization state and polymer solubility switching. Completely different mechanisms producing a generic mathematical form. Generator's own self-critique flagged this as potentially decorative — confirmed.
critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: N/A — killed. The V-shape is a generic mathematical form arising whenever two competing pH-dependent processes operate. No mechanistic connection exists between glass network hydrolysis and drug ionization/polymer solubility.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: Saturation Index-Guided Crystallization Risk Assessment for Supersaturated Drug Solutions
critiqued_hypotheses.key_attack: Activity-corrected supersaturation is NOT new in pharma. The MFAD supersaturation expression (2019) already incorporates activity coefficients. Errors of 190%+ from simplified supersaturation calculations are already documented. The geochemical SI framing (SI = log(a/a_eq)) is mathematically equivalent to log(S_corrected) — a logarithmic transformation, not a conceptual advance. Additionally, crystallization nucleation is stochastic/kinetic, not purely thermodynamically predictable.
critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: Survives as wounded because systematic geochemical SI formalism is not standard practice in pharma ASD development. However, the core insight (use activities not concentrations) is already known. Adds formalization value, not paradigm shift. Crystallization kinetics may dominate over thermodynamic driving force.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: Geochemical Reactive Transport Modeling Predicts In Vivo ASD Dissolution Under GI Transit
critiqued_hypotheses.key_attack: PRIOR ART EXISTS. Yu et al. 1996 developed the 'dispersion model' for intestinal drug absorption using the advection-dispersion-reaction framework — exactly what this hypothesis proposes. Yu & Amidon 2007 published explicit application of the convection-dispersion equation to oral drug absorption modeling. DFCAT model uses 126 ODEs for spatially resolved dissolution-transport-absorption. Existing PBPK tools (GastroPlus, Simcyp) already implement reactive transport models adapted for GI biology. The hypothesis claims novelty that does not exist.
critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: N/A — killed. Classic hallucination-as-novelty: the generator's parametric knowledge of pharma modeling was incomplete, leading to the false belief that reactive transport modeling had never been applied to GI drug dissolution. It has been standard since 1996.

critic_questions.question: Under what specific ASD dissolution regime (drug loading, polymer type, particle size) would surface-reaction kinetics dominate over diffusion? This determines whether TST is applicable at all.
How does the passivation model handle polymer erosion/dissolution that breaks parabolic kinetics? Glass rinds are permanent; polymer layers are transient. What boundary conditions apply?
How do you address the dimensionality problem — drug structural diversity is orders of magnitude greater than silicate glass compositional space. How many parameters would the regression require?
Given that the MFAD supersaturation expression (2019) already incorporates activity coefficients in pharmaceutical crystallization, what does the geochemical SI framing add beyond terminology?

meta_critique_notes: Kill rate 42.9% (3/7) within healthy range. Strongest kill: H1.7 (prior art since Yu et al. 1996). Most controversial survival: H1.1 — the diffusion-vs-surface-reaction question is the key uncertainty. All 7 hypotheses received multiple web searches. Key citation issues: (1) Gin et al. 2015 published in Nature Communications not Nature Materials, (2) spring-parachute coined by Guzmán 2007 not Ting 2018, (3) Sun et al. 2016 F-H chi citation unverified. The wounded hypotheses (H1.2, H1.4, H1.6) share a common pattern: real but shallow mathematical analogies that may not translate to mechanistic insight. H1.1 is the pipeline's strongest candidate — genuinely novel with testable predictions, despite mechanism concerns.
